description:
You are investigating a corporate espionage case where the suspect may have selectively deleted files. Perform a differential analysis comparing allocated vs. deleted files on the evidence image to determine if the deletion was systematic or incidental.

Your mission:
1. Create a forensic case named 'Deletion_Differential_2024' with case number 'INV-DIFF-006' in Autopsy.
2. Add /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd as a Disk Image data source. Enable ONLY the 'File Type Identification' ingest module.
3. After ingest, examine both allocated and deleted files in the file views. Exclude NTFS system metadata files (e.g., $MFT, $LogFile, $Bitmap, files starting with '$').
4. Write a differential analysis report to /home/ga/Reports/deletion_differential.txt containing exactly these sections in this order:
   ALLOCATED_FILE_COUNT: <N>
   DELETED_FILE_COUNT: <N>
   TOTAL_USER_FILES: <N>
   DELETION_RATIO: <N.NN> (deleted count / allocated count)

   ALLOCATED_FILES:
   <filename>|<size_bytes>|<extension>
   ...

   DELETED_FILES:
   <filename>|<size_bytes>|<extension>
   ...

   TYPE_DISTRIBUTION_ALLOCATED:
   <extension>: <N> files
   ...

   TYPE_DISTRIBUTION_DELETED:
   <extension>: <N> files
   ...

   DELETION_PATTERN_ASSESSMENT: <SYSTEMATIC|INCIDENTAL|INCONCLUSIVE>
   ASSESSMENT_JUSTIFICATION: <brief explanation>

5. Write a summary to /home/ga/Reports/deletion_summary.txt with:
   CASE_NUMBER: INV-DIFF-006
   IMAGE_FILE: ntfs_undel.dd
   FILESYSTEM: NTFS
   ALLOCATED_FILE_COUNT: <N>
   DELETED_FILE_COUNT: <N>
   DELETION_RATIO: <N.NN>
   TYPES_ONLY_IN_DELETED: <comma-separated list or 'None'>
   TYPES_ONLY_IN_ALLOCATED: <comma-separated list or 'None'>
   DELETION_PATTERN_ASSESSMENT: <SYSTEMATIC|INCIDENTAL|INCONCLUSIVE>
